Blueprint II: JetBlue To Revive Its Iconic Special Livery On An Airbus A220-300

The carrier is set to make some major changes.

JetBlue's revival of its iconic Blueprint livery on the A220-300 signals a strategic brand reinvestment amid fleet modernization and competitive pressure in the Northeast market.

Air Canada's New Very High-Capacity 440-Seat Boeing 777s [Full Route List & Map]

The reduction from 450 seats is notable. See where they'll fly...

Air Canada's 440-seat configuration reduces the original 450-seat plan, signaling operational constraints or cabin comfort prioritization that may influence industry-wide seating density decisions on widebodies.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The World’s Most Advanced In-Flight Entertainment Systems

A look at why some airlines are considered to have the best in-flight entertainment and how they are rushing to roll out in-flight internet access.

Airlines investing in premium IFE systems are using integrated connectivity and personalized content delivery as competitive differentiators to justify premium pricing in an increasingly commoditized market.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Pilot ejects safely after US Air Force F-35 crash in Nevada

A pilot ejected safely from a US Air Force F-35 after reporting trouble maneuvering before the jet crashed…...

The F-35's ejection system performed as designed despite the aircraft's loss, highlighting critical safety redundancy in fifth-generation fighter operations.

See AeroTime Hub →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The McDonnell Douglas MD-11's Flight Deck

The McDonnell Douglas MD-11's cockpit represented a pivotal moment in aviation history where analog transitioned into digital.

The MD-11's glass cockpit pioneered automation standards that modern commercial aircraft still inherit, making its design legacy foundational to current flight deck technology.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically document why specific controls were selected and justify acceptance of remaining risks, creating an auditable decision trail.

Airport security lines eased at several major US airports on March 31, 2026, after Transportation Security Administration (TSA)…...

TSA staffing disruptions demonstrated how security operations directly impact passenger flow, signaling vulnerabilities in airport operational resilience during labor disputes.

Premium economy's growing adoption signals carriers can capture mid-tier revenue by offering incremental comfort improvements at substantially higher margins than economy.

Same Seats, Different Branding: Alaska Airlines Unveils Boeing 787-9 Business Class Suites

It comes as the airline is set to launch Seattle–Rome flights and expand long-haul service worldwide.

Alaska Airlines' 787-9 deployment signals competitive positioning in premium long-haul markets, leveraging existing business-class infrastructure to compete against legacy carriers on international routes.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

6 Boeing Aircraft That Changed Commercial Aviation Forever

From Boeing's first commercial aircraft launched in 1933 to the modern 787 and 777X families, these aircraft were instrumental in shaping aviation.

Boeing's lineage from the 1933 247 through today's 787/777X demonstrates how aircraft design evolution—pressurization, jet propulsion, composite materials—directly enabled the expansion of global aviation networks and commercial viability.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway exit geometry at LAX creates specific confusion patterns that disproportionately affect pilots unfamiliar with the airport's layout, indicating a systemic design vulnerability rather than isolated pilot error.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Delta Air Lines Partners With Amazon Leo To Bring New High-Speed Wifi To 500 Aircraft

As airlines compete for seamless digital experiences, Delta's partnership with Amazon's satellite network marks a significant leap in onboard Wi-Fi.

Delta's satellite-based connectivity shifts competitive advantage from ground infrastructure to orbital assets, enabling consistent coverage across oceanic routes where traditional broadband fails.

From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

Eastern Iowa Airport (CID) is a medium-sized multi-use airport located south of Cedar Rapids, Iowa. Traffic includes basic training aircraft up through air carrier aircraft. Flight schools often use...

CID's multi-use infrastructure supporting everything from training aircraft to air carriers demonstrates how regional airports can serve dual roles in both pilot development and commercial operations.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

How Airbus Aircraft Noses Differ To Boeing's

Why do aircraft made by Boeing feature slightly different noses to those made by Airbus?

Nose shape differences between Airbus and Boeing reflect distinct design philosophies in cockpit visibility, aerodynamics, and manufacturing processes rather than functional requirements.

For more than a century, Switlik Survival Products has engineered customizable aviation survival solutions trusted by pilots and first responders worldwide.

Switlik's century-long specialization in customizable survival gear represents a critical niche where reliability directly impacts crew and passenger outcomes across diverse aviation operations.

General aviation aircraft like Skyhawks remain operational fixtures at busy facilities alongside premium business jets, highlighting sustained demand across GA market segments.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Pentagon Considers Anti-Drone Lasers in Washington

Reported drone activity near Fort McNair has prompted review of counter-UAS system.

Pentagon's consideration of directed-energy weapons for airspace defense near Washington D.C. signals potential expansion of counter-UAS technology deployment in civilian airspace coordination zones.

See AVweb →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation levels can simultaneously address capacity constraints and environmental impact, bridging the gap between growing demand and sustainability requirements.

See MDPI →

How Aviation Specialties Unlimited sets the standard for night vision training.

ASU's experiential training methodology establishes a replicable framework that could raise night vision proficiency standards across military and commercial aviation operations.

TSA Paychecks Ease Airport Delays

Security lines shorten at several major airports, though the shutdown continues to raise staffing and pay concerns.

While temporary TSA staffing improvements reduce immediate congestion, unresolved compensation issues threaten to reignite screening delays if workforce retention deteriorates further.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Godspeed, Artemis II!

NASA astronaut Jessica Meir took this photo of an Artemis program patch floating in the International Space Station’s cupola. She posted it on X on March 30, 2026, with the following caption: “Our work on the @Space_Station has provided the foundation to explore further, preparing us to return...

ISS microgravity research directly validates life support and human performance protocols essential for sustained lunar missions beyond Earth orbit.

Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

EASA: Narrow Mideast Corridors Increase Operational Risk

European regulators say conflict-related airspace restrictions are increasing congestion and risks.

Compressed routing through conflict zones forces aircraft into narrower flight paths, compounding collision risks and delaying schedules beyond direct conflict avoidance impacts.

Delta Air Lines has selected Amazon Leo over SpaceX’s Starlink to power its future inflight Wi-Fi starting with…...

Delta's choice of Amazon Leo signals a shift toward diversifying satellite connectivity providers, reducing reliance on Starlink's near-monopoly in commercial aviation.

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry

In two recent studies, researchers used Landsat data to fill key gaps in our knowledge of reservoir structure and dynamics.

Satellite remote sensing capabilities demonstrated through Landsat data provide cost-effective alternatives to traditional ground surveys for monitoring critical water infrastructure—a capability increasingly relevant for aerospace applications in environmental monitoring.

See NASA →
